input.background:
Recall the quality-control study in Example 7.5, in which we tested to determine whether a cereal box filling machine was deviating from the specified mean fill of μ=12 ounces. The test setup is repeated here:

H₀: μ=12
Hₐ: μ≠12 (i.e., μ<12 or μ>12)

Test statistic: z=(x̄−12)/σₓ̄
Rejection region: z<-1.96 or z>1.96 for α=.05; z<-2.575 or z>2.575 for α=.01

Note that two rejection regions have been specified corresponding to values of α=.05 and α=.01, respectively. Assume that n=100 and s=.5.
input.question: Suppose the machine is underfilling the boxes by an average of .1 ounce (i.e., μ=11.9). Calculate the values of β corresponding to the two rejection regions. Discuss the relationship between the values of α and β.

output.answer:
For α=.05:

Border values of x̄:

x̄₀ₗ = μ₀ - 1.96σₓ̄ ≈ μ₀ - 1.96(s/√n) = 12 - 1.96(0.5/10) = 11.902
x̄₀ᵤ = μ₀ + 1.96σₓ̄ ≈ μ₀ + 1.96(s/√n) = 12 + 1.96(0.5/10) = 12.098

Convert to z-values in the alternative distribution with μₐ=11.9:

zₗ = (x̄₀ₗ - μₐ)/σₓ̄ ≈ (11.902 - 11.9)/0.05 = 0.04
zᵤ = (x̄₀ᵤ - μₐ)/σₓ̄ ≈ (12.098 - 11.9)/0.05 = 3.96

Using Table II in Appendix D:

Area between z=0 and z=0.04 = 0.0160
Area between z=0 and z=3.96 ≈ 0.5 (off the scale of Table II)
Area between z=0.04 and z=3.96 ≈ 0.5 - 0.0160 = 0.4840

Thus, β ≈ 0.4840 for α=.05.

For α=.01:

Border values of x̄:

x̄₀ₗ = 12 - 2.575(0.5/10) = 11.871
x̄₀ᵤ = 12 + 2.575(0.5/10) = 12.129

Convert to z-values in the alternative distribution with μₐ=11.9:

zₗ = (x̄₀ₗ - μₐ)/σₓ̄ ≈ (11.871 - 11.9)/0.05 = -0.58
zᵤ = (x̄₀ᵤ - μₐ)/σₓ̄ ≈ (12.129 - 11.9)/0.05 = 4.58

Area between z=-0.58 and z=4.58 ≈ 0.2190 + 0.5 = 0.7190

Thus, β ≈ 0.7190 for α=.01.

Relationship between α and β:

As α decreases (from 0.05 to 0.01), β increases (from 0.4840 to 0.7190). This is a general property: decreasing α increases β, and vice versa.

meta_info.caption: The Power of a Test -Quality-Control Study
meta_info.chapter: Inferences Based on a Single Sample
